VC-01HIGHHeadlines & Buttons
CTA Reads Like a Setup Step After a Variant Is Selected

Element

#ProductSubmitButton-template--20109859586113__main

Observation

The product has "Polished Tips" visibly selected, but the primary purchase button still says "SELECT COLOR." On a $1,649.99 PDP, that makes the button feel disabled or incomplete instead of confirming the next buying action.

Recommendation

Once a color is selected, change the button to "Add to Cart - $1,649.99" or "Add Polished Tips to Cart." If a required option is missing, show the missing option directly above the button instead of leaving the CTA ambiguous.

Why this matters

Specific CTA labels reduce uncertainty because shoppers scan buttons out of context; generic or misleading labels cause users to hesitate before committing.

Source

CTA Design And Placement
GOLDOpen primary source ↗
VC-02MEDIUMHeadlines & Buttons
Primary CTA Contrast Is Weaker Than the Header Search and Navigation

Element

#ProductSubmitButton-template--20109859586113__main

Observation

The CTA uses a pale peach fill with white text, while the header search, vehicle selector, and black navigation area carry stronger contrast. The purchase action does not visually dominate the product module.

Recommendation

Use the brand blue or a high-contrast orange with dark text, keep the button filled, and reserve this treatment only for the primary purchase action. The button should be the strongest color block in the right column.

Why this matters

CTA color is not about a universal best color; the button must contrast with its local surroundings enough to win attention.

Source

CTA Design And Placement
SILVEROpen primary source ↗
TC-01HIGHTrust & Credibility
No Reviews or Star Rating Near a $1,649 Product Decision

Element

#ProductInfo-template--20109859586113__main (title/price area)

Observation

The visible product module shows title, color, price, BNPL, quantity, and CTA, but no star rating, review count, verified-buyer language, or review anchor. This leaves a high-ticket automotive purchase without social proof at the moment of evaluation.

Recommendation

Add a review row directly under the title: star rating, review count, and a "Read reviews" anchor. If this specific SKU has few reviews, show verified brand/category reviews nearby and mark the product-specific count honestly.

Why this matters

Reviews matter more for expensive products because higher perceived risk increases reliance on social proof.

Source

Trust And Credibility
GOLDOpen primary source ↗
TC-02MEDIUMTrust & Credibility
Fitment and Return Confidence Are Below the Purchase Zone

Element

#ProductInfo-template--20109859586113__main

Observation

The page has a "Select your vehicle" utility in the header and specifications lower on the page, but the purchase zone does not state "Fits 2016-2018 Focus RS," return terms, warranty, install support, or what is included. Buyers have to infer confidence from scattered content.

Recommendation

Add a compact trust strip below the CTA: "Fits 2016-2018 Focus RS," "Hardware included," "Secure checkout," "Returns/warranty details," and "Free shipping eligibility." Link each item to the relevant details section.

Why this matters

Guarantees and confidence cues work best when framed as positive promises near the decision point, not buried after the shopper has already hesitated.

Source

Trust And Credibility
BRONZEOpen primary source ↗
PR-01MEDIUMPrice & Offers
Compare-At Price Does Not Explain the Savings

Element

div.price.h3-size

Observation

The page shows $1,649.99 next to a struck $1,847.99 price, but it does not state the dollar savings, percent savings, or whether the crossed-out value is MSRP, previous AWDMods price, or manufacturer list price.

Recommendation

Add a clear savings label such as "Save $198 (11%) vs. MSRP" only if the anchor is accurate. If the anchor is not MSRP, label it honestly as "Was" or remove it.

Why this matters

Anchors shape price judgments, but unexplained anchors can look arbitrary on high-ticket products.

Source

Pricing Psychology
GOLDOpen primary source ↗
PR-02HIGHPrice & Offers
Free Shipping Claim Is Not Confirmed at Product Level

Element

promo bar + #ProductInfo-template--20109859586113__main

Observation

The top promo says "FREE SHIPPING on most orders $75+ -- Contiguous US only," but the product module never confirms whether this heavy exhaust qualifies, what shipping method applies, or whether oversized freight exceptions exist.

Recommendation

Under the price or CTA, add a product-specific line: "Qualifies for free contiguous-US shipping" or "Shipping calculated because oversized." Do not rely only on the global promo bar for an expensive, physically large part.

Why this matters

Unexpected shipping costs are a top controllable abandonment reason; product-level clarity prevents late-stage cost surprise.

Source

Free Shipping
GOLDOpen primary source ↗
CS-01HIGHSearch Findability
Product Name Is Rendered as H2 Instead of the Main H1

Element

div.product__title h2.b-main-title.h3

Observation

The captured DOM contains no H1. The visible product title is an H2, so the page is missing a clear main heading for accessibility, page structure, and search-result interpretation.

Recommendation

Render the product title as the page's single H1. Keep visual styling identical if needed, but correct the semantic element.

Why this matters

A PDP needs one clear primary heading that identifies the product for users, assistive technology, and search engines.

Source

Title Formulas SERP Psychology
BRONZEOpen primary source ↗
CS-02HIGHSearch Findability
Description Copy Contains Typos and Weak Benefit Framing

Element

div.content-truncator__content.product__description

Observation

The description includes visible copy errors: "so would this would" and "thst." It also repeats product-name phrasing before explaining the buyer outcome. For a premium exhaust, the copy should make the sound, fitment, material, and included hardware feel confidence-building.

Recommendation

Rewrite the opening paragraph around outcomes first: aggressive Borla ATAK sound, direct bolt-on fitment for 2016-2018 Focus RS, stainless construction, included hardware/instructions, and tip-finish choice. Then list specs below.

Why this matters

Buyers evaluate outcomes before specifications; typo-heavy copy makes a premium part feel less credible.

Source

Benefit First Descriptions
SILVEROpen primary source ↗
CS-03MEDIUMSearch Findability
Product Metadata Omits Reviews, Return Policy, and Shipping Details

Element

script[type="application/ld+json"]

Observation

The page has Product JSON-LD with name, image, description, SKU, brand, and offers. It does not include aggregateRating/review data, shippingDetails, or MerchantReturnPolicy fields.

Recommendation

Extend the product metadata for Google's merchant listing path with accurate aggregateRating/review data when available, shippingDetails, and return policy data. Keep values synchronized with the Shopify product/feed source.

Why this matters

Ecommerce PDPs are merchant listing pages; richer product metadata supports enhanced search listings and AI-shopping comparison surfaces.

Source

Schema Product Markup
GOLDOpen primary source ↗
